//: Contact registry (Phase 10b, presentation_layer_narrative.md §4.2). contact_id -> display metadata for the Burner Phone's threaded messages. 'unknown' is the reserved fallback thread for events an authored batch never tags with "contact" -- always present (ContactRegistry synthesizes it even if a future edit drops this row). The named contacts group the shipped gritty events (Sonnet 5, 10b tagging pass): coach_reyes carries the on-field/clubhouse arc, agent_diaz carries league/legal/health correspondence, girlfriend_jess carries the pro-era romance/marriage/family arc (age > 20), family_mom carries the pre-16 heir-interest beats. family_mom + family_dad are the HS-2 household threads -- CreateAvatar now inserts two real parent NPC Players rows; the HS-5 hs_dating_events.json batch finally gives family_dad content (the parental-approval beat). hs_sweetheart is HS-5's own pre-marriage dating-funnel thread (role: partner, gender-neutral) -- deliberately distinct from girlfriend_jess so the two romance arcs (HS vs pro) never share a contact thread or collide on the met_someone age>20 gate. The syndicate/arrest arc stays on 'unknown' by design -- those events are deliberately anonymous threats, not named relationships. family_kid is child_rearing_events.json's thread (arc-agnostic -- shared by HS- and pro-era children alike, since hs_has_child is set by both hs_baby_born and child_is_born) for the household's day-to-day parenting beats and the school's own calls home. coach_hs (Coach Malone) and hs_friend (Marcus) are the two new HS onboarding threads (hs_onboarding_events.json) -- coach_hs is the HS varsity coach, deliberately distinct from coach_reyes (the pro clubhouse coach) exactly as hs_sweetheart is kept distinct from girlfriend_jess, so the HS and pro arcs never share a contact thread; hs_friend is the classmate/buddy voice for the social/rival/homecoming beats. getting_started is the mechanical 'how to play' thread, deliberately distinct from every narrative contact so coaching copy never pollutes a relationship thread. Its 4-event tutorial batch (getting_started_events.json) was RETIRED 2026-07-11 in favor of the day-1 TutorialOverlay (docs/design/onboarding_tutorial_overlay.md, which supersedes day1_phone_tutorial.md's delivery mechanism); the contact row deliberately stays as a dormant hint-thread hook per that doc's own S6 -- no shipped event currently tags it, which is harmless (an empty thread never renders). The original design doc proposed a new role:'system'; ContactJson's role parser is a closed switch (throws on an unrecognized string), so this ships on role:'unknown' instead -- zero ContactRegistry.cs touch. the_bookie ('Sal') is DIRT-1's one new named thread (dirt_underworld_events.json, docs/design/criminal_underworld_content_arc.md §6/§5) -- the gambling-debt spiral's recurring, FAMILIAR associate, deliberately distinct from the syndicate/arrest arc's anonymous 'unknown' thread (a debt collector you know by name vs. faceless enforcers). Ships on role:'friend' (already valid, the hs_friend precedent) -- zero ContactRegistry.cs touch, same discipline as every other batch here.
